title: Deploy ah check to all charly projects with openspec specs
description:
Why: Only 2/27 projects have .espectacular/ configured. 17 have openspec specs but no verification layer.

What: Run ah init in each target project. This creates .espectacular/config.toml and stubs contracts for each spec scenario.

Targets by batch:
  Batch 1: dont, wai (most active projects)
  Batch 2: pretender, atril, fotos
  Batch 3: khipu, miblioteca, nayra, paranoid, rizomas, ruta, superficies, tRAGar

Acceptance:
  - ah check exits 0 in each project after init
  - All changes committed and pushed per project

Source session: 019f72d1-e2bf-7d6f-a707-403228d4ee3b

title: Verify ah binary availability in pi bash execution context
description:
Why: EDGE-001 from the Rule-of-5 review. If pi's bash tool spawns with a cleaned PATH (no ~/.cargo/bin), ah would not be found.

Investigation: Already confirmed during ticket creation. ah IS available:
- which ah points to /var/home/sasha/.cargo/bin/ah
- ah --version reports ah 0.2.2
- ~/.cargo/bin IS in pi's PATH

Remaining Work:
1. Document this finding in project AGENTS.md or wiki
2. Add a PATH check to the pi extension as a safeguard
3. Verify on machines without ~/.cargo/bin (fresh installs) and add install steps if needed

Source session: 019f72d1-e2bf-7d6f-a707-403228d4ee3b

title: Add ah:managed block to AGENTS.md in all openspec projects
description:
Why: Only 2/27 projects have the ah:managed block in AGENTS.md. Without it, agents don't know to run ah check.

What: Insert the managed block (ah:managed:start/end markers) into each target project's AGENTS.md. The block documents: ah check, ah check --changes, ah init, ah doctor, ah explain, ah signals.

Targets: dont, wai, pretender, atril, fotos, khipu, miblioteca, nayra, paranoid, rizomas, ruta, superficies, tRAGar

Source session: 019f72d1-e2bf-7d6f-a707-403228d4ee3b

title: Create pi custom tool extension for ah check
description:
Why: No pi extension means agents must discover ah via bash. pi supports pi.registerTool() for named tool discovery.

What: Create ~/.pi/agent/extensions/ah-tool.ts that registers ah_check as a named tool with: name ah_check, description for spec-test correspondence validation, parameters: changes (optional), project (optional). Executes ah check via child_process.

Also consider registering separate tools for: ah doctor, ah signals, ah explain.

Reference: pi docs examples/extensions/dynamic-tools.ts and tools.ts.

Source session: 019f72d1-e2bf-7d6f-a707-403228d4ee3b

title: Extract testaruda ah check workflow as replicable pattern
description:
Why: testaruda proves agents use ah check when configured. The edit -> ah check -> fix -> ah check -> commit loop is the pattern to replicate.

What: Extract the workflow from testaruda pi sessions. Key sessions to analyze:
  - 2026-07-08T15-35-34-510Z (heavy ah usage)
  - 2026-07-16T01-38-10-034Z (recent workflow)

Output: Document in docs/agent-workflow.md as a replicable pattern showing:
  1. How agents discover ah check (via AGENTS.md managed block)
  2. Typical usage patterns
  3. How findings are interpreted
  4. Commit workflow with ah check as gate

Source session: 019f72d1-e2bf-7d6f-a707-403228d4ee3b

title: Bootstrap this repo's .espectacular and prove ah check end-to-end
description:
Epic acceptance for espectacular-ol7 still requires ah check to run against this repository's own .espectacular config and contracts.

Observed gap:
- cargo run --bin ah -- check currently fails because .espectacular/config.toml is missing

Needed work:
- run ah init or equivalent bootstrap for this repo
- stage or author the minimal deployed contracts needed for the repo
- make ah check pass end-to-end from repo root
- close espectacular-ol7 once acceptance is satisfied

title: ah Q11: integration tests and openspec validate --strict

title: ah Q9: ah explain subcommand (compile-enforced playbook)
description:
Section 9 of add-quality-measurement-and-adapters (tasks.md 9.1–9.13).

Covers:
- Compile-time enforcement of variant-body coverage (proc macro or build.rs assertion)
- Markdown output for every FindingKind variant (including quality-mutation/property/snapshot) and every SuggestedAction
- General topics: workflow, supersession, archetypes, progressive-enablement
- --json output (provisional schema)
- --list stable output; unknown-topic error
- Each adapter contributes its own topic; explain registry is single source of truth
acceptance_criteria: ah explain <topic> prints markdown body for every FindingKind variant and SuggestedAction; compile-time enforcement (proc macro or build.rs) fails the build if any variant is missing a body; --json emits provisional schema; --list emits stable sorted output; unknown topic exits non-zero with did-you-mean.
close_reason: Implemented ah explain: 31-topic registry (17 finding kinds, 7 suggested actions, 4 general, 3 adapter), compile-time enforcement via exhaustive match, --list/--json/did-you-mean; 25 new tests (14 unit + 11 integration), all 200 tests passing

title: ah Q2: adapter trait and dispatch
description:
Section 2 of add-quality-measurement-and-adapters (tasks.md 2.1–2.5).

Covers:
- Adapter trait in src/adapters/mod.rs (detect, invoke, normalize)
- Detection precedence chain
- Adapter dispatch from config in gate runner
- Detection/invocation isolation
close_reason: Merged into 6nm.4 per review SCOPE-002: adapter trait is best defined by its first concrete use (pytest); separate trait ticket was a horizontal layer without a consumer.

title: ah Q1: finding schema extension (agent-action fields)
description:
Section 1 of add-quality-measurement-and-adapters (tasks.md 1.1–1.7).

Covers:
- Every finding carries suggested_action, playbook_command, scenario_prose (when applicable)
- Extend schemas/check-output.schema.json
- Deterministic sort by (spec_path, scenario_id, kind)
- summary.counts_by_kind in envelope
- Centralize finding construction so agent-action fields cannot be omitted
design:
Why these fields: agents consuming the JSON envelope need a deterministic next step (suggested_action) and a directly-runnable command (playbook_command) to act without a roundtrip to docs. counts_by_kind in summary lets dashboards aggregate without re-walking findings.

Alternatives considered:
- Free-form 'hint' strings — rejected: too easy to omit, can't be statically enforced.
- Separate suggestion sidecar file — rejected: breaks the single-envelope contract that the gate is built on.
- Build suggested_action lazily in the consumer — rejected: requires every consumer to embed the same heuristics; finding source is the only place with full context.

Constraint: centralized Finding constructor — every emit-site must go through one builder so missing fields fail to compile, not at runtime.
acceptance_criteria: Every finding emitted by ah check carries suggested_action (enum), playbook_command (string|null), and scenario_prose where applicable; schemas/check-output.schema.json includes these fields and summary.counts_by_kind in the envelope; findings sort deterministically by (spec_path, scenario_id, kind); centralized Finding constructor compile-prevents omission of agent-action fields.

title: ah Q0: custom runner envelope schema (gates Q3–Q6)
description:
Section 0 of add-quality-measurement-and-adapters (tasks.md 0.1–0.5).

Resolves the proposal's named blocker. Schema must define:
- schemas/custom-runner.schema.json exists, valid JSON Schema
- Envelope fields: exit_code (int, required), passed (bool, required), findings (array of full finding objects, required)
- Empty findings + exit_code 0 is valid pass case
- Cross-reference from schemas/check-output.schema.json so both schemas share the finding object definition

Blocks: Q3, Q4, Q5, Q6.
close_reason: Folded into 6nm.7 per review SCOPE-001: envelope schema is only consumed by the custom runner plugin (Q3/Q4/Q5 don't use it); keeping schema with its sole consumer eliminates a horizontal-layer ticket.

title: ah S4: lifecycle commands (init, doctor, scenario new/supersede, archive, upgrade)
description:
Section 4 of add-spec-assertions (tasks.md 4.1–4.19).

Covers:
- ah init: .espectacular/config.toml, .espectacular/AGENTS.md, top-level AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md managed blocks; idempotent; refuse without openspec/; stub empty TOML for existing deployed scenarios
- Hook detection precedence (lefthook → prek → no raw git hook fallback)

- ah archive <change>: move staged contracts, refuse collisions/pre-OpenSpec-archive orphans

title: ah S2: test execution and stable JSON output
description:
Section 2 of add-spec-assertions (tasks.md 2.1–2.11).

Covers:

Implement OpenSpec proposal add-spec-assertions: ah CLI as deterministic correspondence gate for OpenSpec scenarios.

Sections tracked as child tickets:

notes: Epic acceptance still needs repo-local bootstrap for ah check end-to-end; follow-up tracked in espectacular-9si.

title: Tracer bullet: one dont rejection signal end-to-end through espectacular to wai
description:
Prove the feedback loop works for one concrete signal before generalizing. This ticket cuts vertically through dont → espectacular → wai:

1. **dont** emits one structured rejection event (e.g., ungrounded-claim rule fires) as JSON to a known location
2. **espectacular** reads the event, maps it to an openspec assertion, and produces a drift signal
3. **wai** consumes the drift signal and surfaces it during wai status or wai doctor

## Done criteria

- A working demo: run dont on a file with an ungrounded claim → espectacular picks up the rejection → wai doctor shows a drift warning
- The event schema is documented (even if minimal)
- Each tool's integration point is a single function behind a feature flag

## Scope constraints

- One signal type only (ungrounded-claim rejection from dont)
- One language only (Go or Rust — whichever has an existing openspec scenario to test against)
- No persistence layer — file-based events are sufficient for the tracer bullet
- No CI integration yet

## Starting points

- **dont:** src/envelope.rs:72-105 (ErrorResult struct) — extend to optionally write a structured event file
- **espectacular:** new — minimal binary that reads an event file and emits a drift signal
- **wai:** src/commands/doctor.rs:2100-2180 (artifact lock checks) — extend to also check for espectacular drift signals

## Downstream

Once this works, the three generalization tickets become unblocked:
- dont-nwck — generalize rejection event capture
- pretender-5rk — add pretender violation events to the same pipeline
- wai-f0dv — generalize drift consumption beyond espectacular signals
acceptance_criteria: A demo run: dont detects an ungrounded claim → writes a structured rejection event → espectacular reads the event and emits a drift signal → wai doctor surfaces the drift. Event schema is documented (even minimally); each integration point sits behind a feature flag; one signal type (ungrounded-claim) in one language is sufficient.
close_reason: Tracer bullet complete: dont emits rejection events (DONT_EMIT_EVENTS=1), espectacular reads them via ah signals, wai doctor surfaces them (WAI_DONT_SIGNALS=1). Full pipeline verified end-to-end.

title: Audit pi sessions for spec-validation patterns across charly projects
description:
Why: Need to know what agents do instead of ah check when validating specs.

What: Search all charly project pi sessions for:
  - openspec validate calls
  - Manual spec-to-test comparisons (reading spec + test files in succession)
  - User messages requesting validation and what tool the agent reaches for
  - cargo test / just test calls that appear to be spec-validation proxies

Output: Brief report documenting pain points as evidence for the pi extension ticket (espectacular-c9v).

Source session: 019f72d1-e2bf-7d6f-a707-403228d4ee3b

title: Add ah check validate recipe to justfiles across charly projects
description:
Why: Most projects lack a just validate recipe. Agents use just test as a proxy for spec verification. Adding a just validate recipe makes spec-test correspondence checking as easy as running tests.

What: Add to each target project's justfile:
  validate:\n    ah check

Targets: dont, wai, pretender, atril, fotos, khipu, miblioteca, nayra, paranoid, rizomas, ruta, superficies, tRAGar

Source session: 019f72d1-e2bf-7d6f-a707-403228d4ee3b

title: ah check exits code 1 silently on validation failure
description:
`ah check` exits with code 1 and produces zero stdout/stderr output when validation fails. Contrast with `ah doctor` which prints actionable diagnostics (orphan contracts, version drift, etc.).

Steps to reproduce:
1. `ah check` in a repo with an orphan contract (e.g., testaruda/.espectacular/)
2. Observe: exit code 1, no output

Expected: `ah check` should print which contracts or validation rules failed, like `ah doctor` does. A silent exit with no diagnostic makes debugging impossible for CI pipelines.
close_reason: Fix: ah check now outputs human-readable diagnostics instead of silent JSON

title: F1: espectacular — adopt shared JSON envelope crate
description:
Part of the F1 epic: Extract shared JSON envelope crate from dont and adopt suite-wide.

Scope for espectacular (ah): Replace bare "findings" key vocabulary in --json responses with the shared envelope. Ensure "ah doctor --json", "ah explain --json", "ah report --json" wrap data in {ok, envelope_version, cli_version, envelope_kind, data, warnings, hints, meta}.

Files: espectacular/ -- after shared crate is released, add it as a dependency and wrap all --json output.

Dependencies: Blocked on dont-2j6o (the F1 epic in dont -- the crate must exist first).

title: F19: espectacular — add coverage job to CI (currently none)
description:
Source: CLI consistency audit F19

Problem: espectacular has no coverage job in CI at all. The suite standard (from dont) uses taiki-e/install-action@cargo-tarpaulin + codecov/codecov-action@v5.

Expected: Add a coverage job to espectacular/.github/workflows/ci.yml, matching dont's pattern:
- Install: taiki-e/install-action@cargo-tarpaulin
- Run: cargo tarpaulin
- Upload: codecov/codecov-action@v5

Files: espectacular/.github/workflows/ci.yml — add coverage job.

Reference: dont/.github/workflows/ci.yml is the canonical template.

Verification: "grep 'tarpaulin' espectacular/.github/workflows/ci.yml" and "grep 'codecov' espectacular/.github/workflows/ci.yml" should both match.

Classification: AFK (agent-friendly).

title: F3/F4: espectacular — standardize --json as global flag, make --version honor --json
description:
Source: CLI consistency audit F3/F4

Problem: espectacular's --json is a per-subcommand bare flag (no short form), present on some subcommands but absent from others (check, archive, type, upgrade, scenario). --version --json is not supported. The suite standard is dont's convention: global -j/--json inherited by all subcommands, with --version --json producing a structured envelope.

Expected: "ah --help" should show -j, --json at the root level. "ah --version --json" should return a JSON envelope (matching dont pattern). All subcommands should inherit --json from the root.

Files: src/main.rs or equivalent — make --json a global clap flag. Add --version --json support.

Reference: dont is the canonical implementation.

Verification: "ah --version --json | jq -e '.ok == true'" should succeed.

Classification: HITL (CLI API change, needs human review).

title: F7: llm.txt documents ### Requirement: as the testable unit; actual discovery key is #### Scenario:
description:
## Parent
Evaluation report: ~/para/projects/astra-zeneca/dev/sandbox/charly-vibes-tools/dev-tooling-evaluation.md §F7

## What to build

`llm.txt` (and `llms.txt`) states:

> **Scenario** — one `### Requirement:` heading; has a slug (the id), a body, and a sidecar contract

This is wrong. The discovery key in `src/openspec.rs` L49 is the `#### Scenario:` prefix — not `### Requirement:`. The `### Requirement:` heading is only a grouping label, not the testable unit.

**Impact:** following the `llm.txt` quick-start produces **zero discovered scenarios** in `ah check`. The tool's own `ah init` correctly generates `#### Scenario:` stubs — confirming the documentation, not the tool, is wrong.

## Files / Systems
- `llm.txt` and/or `llms.txt` — the "Scenario" concept definition and any example spec snippets
- Any other docs that repeat the `### Requirement:` = scenario misstatement

## Fix

Change the Scenario definition in `llm.txt` to:

> **Scenario** — one `#### Scenario:` heading nested under a `### Requirement:` grouping; has a slug (the id), a body, and a sidecar contract

Update any example Markdown snippets to use `#### Scenario:` not `### Requirement:`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `llm.txt` / `llms.txt` correctly documents `#### Scenario:` as the discovery heading
- [ ] A spec following the updated quick-start produces ≥1 scenario in `ah check --dry-run` or `ah check`
- [ ] No other docs in the repo repeat the incorrect `### Requirement:` = scenario claim

---
**CRITICAL: Follow Test Driven Development and Tidy First workflows.**
- Write tests *before* writing implementation code.
- Clean up related code *before* adding new functionality.
acceptance_criteria: llm.txt correctly documents #### Scenario: as the discovery key; following the quick-start produces discovered scenarios in ah check

title: F4: ah init should print the minimal openspec/ directory layout when the directory is absent
description:
## Parent
Evaluation report: ~/para/projects/astra-zeneca/dev/sandbox/charly-vibes-tools/dev-tooling-evaluation.md §F4

## What to build

Running `ah init` in a repo that has no `openspec/` directory exits 1 with an unhelpful error:
```
no openspec/ directory found …; ah init requires an OpenSpec project
```

There is no indication of what the directory should contain, how to create it, or that the `openspec` CLI is a separate tool. First-time users hit a dead end.

## Files / Systems
- `src/commands/init.rs` (or wherever the openspec-directory guard is checked)
- The error message / help text for the missing-directory case

## Fix

When `openspec/` is absent, print the minimal expected layout and the fastest path to creating it:

```
Error: openspec/ directory not found.

ah requires an OpenSpec project. Create the minimal layout:

  openspec/
  └── specs/
      └── my-spec/
          └── spec.md      ← "#### Scenario: …" headings go here

Or install the openspec CLI and run:  openspec init
```

(No code change to the guard logic — only the error message.)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ah init` in a repo without `openspec/` prints the minimal layout and an actionable next step
- [ ] The printed layout matches what `ah check` actually requires (i.e. uses `#### Scenario:` headings)
- [ ] Test for the error message content

---
**CRITICAL: Follow Test Driven Development and Tidy First workflows.**
- Write tests *before* writing implementation code.
- Clean up related code *before* adding new functionality.
acceptance_criteria: ah init without openspec/ prints the required directory layout and a path forward; no bare exit-1

title: Runner should treat zero-tests-matched as contract failure
description:
## Why this exists

When a [[tests.shell]] command uses a cargo test filter that matches no tests (e.g. due to a renamed or deleted test function), cargo test -q exits 0 with '0 tests run'. espectacular interprets exit 0 as PASSED, so the contract silently becomes green with no tests actually executed. This breaks the monitoring guarantee: the contract appears wired but exercises nothing.

## What needs to be done

In the shell test runner (likely src/runner.rs or wherever shell test results are collected), after a test command exits 0, check whether any tests actually ran. If 0 tests ran but exit was 0, emit a new finding kind (e.g. no-tests-ran or test-filter-matched-nothing) rather than treating the contract as passing.

This requires parsing the cargo test -q output line 'test result: ok. N passed' and treating N=0 with exit 0 as a failure condition.

## Acceptance criteria

- ah check emits a finding (not silent pass) when a [[tests.shell]] command exits 0 but runs 0 tests
- Existing contracts with real tests continue to pass normally
- The finding kind is documented in ah explain
close_reason: Implemented: shell tests that exit 0 with cargo-style '0 passed' output now emit no-tests-ran finding; added explain topic; 3 tests cover zero/nonzero/plain-exit cases

title: Implement ah doctor --json output
description:
## Why this exists

The cli spec scenario 'recommendation-finding-is-a-finding-kind-not-a-log-line' requires that ah doctor accepts a --json flag and emits a findings array where each recommendation has kind=recommendation and carries a playbook_command field. Currently ah doctor has no --json flag. The contract espectacular/cli/recommendation-finding-is-a-finding-kind-not-a-log-line.toml cannot be wired to a test until this is implemented.

## What needs to be done

1. Add --json flag to the Doctor CLI subcommand in main.rs
2. Emit a JSON object with a findings array from run_doctor
3. Each DoctorRecommendation should map to a finding with kind=recommendation and a playbook_command field
4. Add a [[tests.shell]] entry to the contract pointing at an integration test that covers this flag

## Acceptance criteria

ah check reports 0 no-tests-declared for recommendation-finding-is-a-finding-kind-not-a-log-line

title: ah scenario new creates stubs that immediately fail ah check
description:
## Why this exists

When ah scenario new creates a contract stub, the generated .toml does not include a [[tests.*]] entry. Because the Contract struct requires tests (no #[serde(default)]), every stub immediately fails ah check with malformed-contract.

This creates a poor workflow: you run ah scenario new, then run ah check out of habit, and get 81 errors — all noise, none of them actionable without knowing the schema.

## What needs to be done

Option A: Add a placeholder [[tests.shell]] comment or empty section to generated stubs so the schema is valid from creation.
Option B: Add #[serde(default)] to the tests field in Contract and treat empty tests as a distinct 'no-contract' state with a separate finding kind (e.g. 'no-tests').
Option C: Have ah scenario new print a reminder to add tests before committing.

Preferred: Option B separates 'file is broken' (malformed-contract) from 'file is a stub' (no-tests), making the distinction actionable.

## Acceptance criteria

Running ah scenario new followed immediately by ah check produces 0 malformed-contract findings for the newly created contract (it may show a different finding like no-tests, but not a confusing parse error).
notes: HITL decision needed before claiming: Option B is recommended (add #[serde(default)] to tests field, emit a no-tests finding kind instead of malformed-contract for stubs). This separates 'broken file' from 'stub not yet wired'. Decide A/B/C before implementing.

title: Possible flake: property_failing_emits_test_failing fails when run with other check tests
description:
## Why this exists

ah check occasionally reports a test-failing finding for the gate spec scenario 'do-not-inspect-test-internals'. The contract (gate/do-not-inspect-test-internals.toml) runs: cargo test -q check::tests::

During one ah check run, check::tests::property_failing_emits_test_failing failed with 'expected test-failing finding'. Running the test in isolation always passes. This is a test isolation issue — the test creates a temp dir repo and spawns a property runner script; running concurrently with other check tests may cause interference.

## What needs to be done

1. Reproduce by running: for i in {1..20}; do cargo test --bin ah -q 'check::tests::' || echo FAILED; done
2. Identify shared state causing the flake (likely temp file paths, env var leakage, or process spawning)
3. Add serial execution or fix root cause

## Acceptance criteria

cargo test --bin ah 'check::tests::' passes 100/100 consecutive runs.
notes:
Acceptance criteria updated: verify with 30 consecutive runs locally:
  for i in $(seq 1 30); do cargo test --bin ah -q 'check::tests::' 2>&1 | grep -E 'FAILED|ok' | tail -1; done
Or via CI. The 100/100 bar in the original description is impractical AFK.

title: malformed-contract error message misleads: says 'invalid TOML' when field is missing
description:
## Why this exists

When a contract file is syntactically valid TOML but missing the required tests field, ah check reports:
  'invalid TOML in /path/to/contract.toml'

This is misleading — the TOML is perfectly valid. The real error is a missing required field.

The root cause is in src/contracts.rs:28 where toml::from_str uses a generic context message:
  .with_context(|| format!("invalid TOML in {toml_path}"))

When serde fails to deserialize (missing field, wrong type, etc.), the context swallows the real error.

Additionally, the playbook guidance for malformed-contract says 'fix the invalid field in the .toml' without identifying which field is wrong, leaving the user to guess.

## What needs to be done

1. In load_contract(), propagate the actual deserialization error (e.g. 'missing field tests') instead of overwriting it with 'invalid TOML in ...'
2. In the malformed-contract playbook entry (explain/), add guidance: 'run ah check --verbose or inspect the error message to identify the missing/invalid field'
3. Consider emitting the specific field name in the finding message field

## Acceptance criteria

When a contract is missing the tests field, ah check message field reads something like:
  'contract missing required field: tests (in path/to/contract.toml)'
rather than the generic 'invalid TOML in ...'.
notes: The malformed-contract explain topic is in src/explain.rs (static MALFORMED_CONTRACT around line 419). The review_and_apply topic at ~line 775 also references malformed-contract and needs updating. Both were updated as part of the 2026-06-18 fix.

title: Wire up adapters spec contracts with test entries
description:
## Why this exists

25 contracts in .espectacular/adapters/ are stubs with no [[tests.*]] entries. ah check reports all 25 as malformed-contract because the Contract struct requires a tests field.

## What needs to be done

For each of the 25 adapter contracts, identify the corresponding Rust test function(s) in src/ and add the appropriate [[tests.cargo]] or [[tests.shell]] entry. The contracts cover: cargo detection, cargo exit normalization, pytest detection/normalization, vitest detection/normalization, custom runner behavior, detection precedence (explicit-config > manifest > environment > source-import), missing-adapter finding, envelope pass/fail logic, and detection source reporting.

## Acceptance criteria

ah check reports 0 malformed-contract findings for the adapters spec.
notes:
Discovery: grep -L 'tests\.' .espectacular/adapters/*.toml | sort
Pattern: .espectacular/cli/archive-staged-contracts.toml (shell entry example)

title: Wire up remaining cli spec contracts with test entries
description:
## Why this exists

20 of 42 contracts in .espectacular/cli/ are stubs with no [[tests.*]] entries. ah check reports these 20 as malformed-contract. The 22 already-wired contracts use [[tests.shell]] with cargo test -q <filter>.

## What needs to be done

For each of the 20 missing cli contracts, identify the corresponding Rust test and add [[tests.shell]] entries following the same pattern as existing contracts. Missing ones include: enable-* commands (cargo, pytest, vitest, mutation, property, snapshot capabilities), explain-* commands, report-* commands, recommendation-finding scenarios, unknown-topic, and list-all-topics.

## Acceptance criteria

ah check reports 0 malformed-contract findings for the cli spec.
notes: Wired up 15 of 20 stubs. Remaining 5 have no backing implementation: recommendation-finding-is-a-finding-kind-not-a-log-line (requires ah doctor --json) and report-coverage-by-spec-and-archetype, report-exits-non-zero-when-scenarios-are-missing-contracts, report-exits-zero-when-coverage-is-complete, report-json-output (require ah report command). These need separate feature tickets.
close_reason: Wired up 15 of 20 cli stubs with test entries; remaining 5 require unimplemented features tracked in espectacular-14e (ah doctor --json) and espectacular-gqi (ah report command)

title: Scaffold mdBook and GitHub Actions publish workflow
description:
Choose and scaffold a documentation framework for the espectacular docs site. mdBook is the natural choice for a Rust CLI project — it produces a fast static site, integrates well with cargo projects, and has good search support.

Tasks:
(1) Add a docs/ directory with book.toml and SUMMARY.md covering the three content sections (installation, concepts, command reference).
(2) Write docs/src/index.md — a one-paragraph project summary with links to the three main sections (landing page).
(3) Add a GitHub Actions workflow at .github/workflows/docs.yml that builds mdBook and deploys to GitHub Pages on push to main.
(4) ⚠ HITL required: after the workflow is merged, a repo admin must go to Settings → Pages → Source: GitHub Actions to activate the site.

Acceptance: push to main triggers the workflow successfully; repo admin enables Pages; site renders at https://charly-vibes.github.io/espectacular/ (or equivalent org URL). Verify with: curl -I https://<org>.github.io/espectacular/ returns 200.
close_reason: mdBook scaffold + GH Actions workflow committed and pushed; stub pages for installation/commands/concepts in place so build passes

title: Write installation and quick-start guide
description: Users need a getting-started page that covers: installing ah (cargo install or binary download), running ah init in an existing openspec project, running ah check for the first time, and interpreting the output. Should include a minimal worked example showing a spec file, a contract stub, and a passing check.
close_reason: full content written, mdBook build passing

title: Write command reference documentation
description:
Document all ah subcommands with their flags, expected output, and exit codes. Commands to cover: ah check, ah check --changes <name>, ah init, ah doctor, ah doctor --enable <adapter>, ah explain <topic>, ah explain --list, ah signals, ah type, ah type <archetype>. Each entry should include: synopsis, description, flags, example invocation, and example output.

Acceptance: every command listed above has a corresponding section; running each command locally produces output that matches the documented examples.
close_reason: full content written, mdBook build passing

title: Write concepts guide (specs, contracts, scenarios, archetypes)
description: Users need a conceptual overview before commands make sense. Topics: what a spec is and where it lives, what a scenario is, what a contract is and the sidecar file convention, what an archetype is (PF/SA/BP/CE/NR), how the gate works, and how adapters map test types to runners. This is the page that explains the mental model.
close_reason: full content written, mdBook build passing

title: User documentation site published to GitHub Pages
description: espectacular has no user-facing documentation site. Users reading the README have no structured guide to getting started, command reference, or concept explanations. We need a documentation site built from source in this repo and auto-published to GitHub Pages on every push to main.

title: Update AGENTS.md managed block for new ah commands
description:
The <!-- ah:managed:start --> block in AGENTS.md only lists four commands (ah check, ah check --changes, ah init, ah doctor). It is missing commands added by add-quality-measurement-and-adapters:

- ah explain <topic> — playbook guidance for finding kinds and suggested actions
- ah doctor --enable <adapter> — write adapter config into .espectacular/config.toml
- ah signals — emit dont drift signals

This block is injected by ah init, so the source template in src/init.rs also needs updating to match. The template is the AH_BLOCK_CONTENT constant at src/init.rs:18.

Verification: run cargo run -- init on a scratch project (with an existing openspec/specs dir and .espectacular/config.toml) and confirm the injected <!-- ah:managed --> block contains all five commands. Also confirm the live AGENTS.md in this repo is updated to match.
close_reason: Updated AH_BLOCK_CONTENT in src/init.rs plus live CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md; verified ah init injects all 7 commands

title: Update README for add-quality-measurement-and-adapters
description:
The README was last updated when add-spec-assertions landed (2026-05-20). It is missing everything introduced by add-quality-measurement-and-adapters:

- ah explain command (absent from commands table)
- ah doctor --enable flag and adapter readiness workflow
- Language adapter dispatch section (pytest/cargo/vitest auto-detection, detection precedence)
- Custom runner protocol (JSON envelope format, config shape)
- Quality signals (quality-mutation, quality-property, quality-snapshot) and how to enable them in config.toml
- New finding kinds: quality-mutation, quality-property, quality-snapshot in the finding kinds table
- counts_by_kind field in the ah check JSON output example
- suggested_action and playbook_command fields on findings
- schemas/custom-runner.schema.json in the Schemas section
- ah signals command
- Fix stale schema paths: config.schema.json and scenario-contract.schema.json now live under schemas/, not openspec/changes/add-spec-assertions/schemas/

The commands table, config.toml example, JSON output example, and finding kinds table all need updating.

Done when: ah explain --list output matches the README explain section; ah doctor --enable flag is documented with example; quality signal config.toml blocks are present and valid TOML; all three new finding kinds appear in the finding kinds table; schema paths in the Schemas section point to schemas/ not openspec/changes/.
close_reason: README updated with all missing content from add-quality-measurement-and-adapters; ETXTBSY flake also fixed as part of pre-push hook failure

v1 stays measure-only; no hard gate on scores.

title: ah Q6: custom runner plugin protocol
description:
Section 6 + Section 0 of add-quality-measurement-and-adapters (tasks.md 0.1–0.5, 6.1–6.5).

Absorbs the Q0 envelope schema (formerly 6nm.1, folded in per review: the envelope is consumed only by the custom runner plugin, so coupling it here keeps schema and consumer in one ticket).

Covers:
- Author schemas/custom-runner.schema.json (envelope: exit_code int required, passed bool required, findings array required; empty findings + exit_code 0 is valid pass)
- Cross-reference from schemas/check-output.schema.json so finding object definition is shared
- Custom adapter (runner = '\''custom'\'') invokes a user-defined executable
- Validate envelope against the schema; map structural violations to envelope_violation findings (test-failing)
- Capture stderr in execution_error findings when the runner exits non-zero
design:
Why fold envelope schema here (formerly Q0 / 6nm.1):
- The envelope is consumed ONLY by the custom runner plugin. Defining it in a separate horizontal-layer ticket created a false 'blocks Q3–Q6' dependency.
- Q3 (pytest), Q4 (cargo), Q5 (vitest) speak their toolchain's native protocol, not the custom envelope.
- Schema-and-consumer in one ticket means one PR ships a working capability, not two PRs that only work together.

Envelope shape rationale:
- exit_code (int, required) — Unix convention; lets shell-only consumers gate without parsing JSON.
- passed (bool, required) — denormalizes the success signal so the gate doesn't infer from exit_code alone; lets runners report 'all tests ran but findings present' (exit 0, passed false).
- findings (array, required) — empty array on green so the schema is always uniform; uses the same finding object as schemas/check-output.schema.json via JSON Schema $ref.

Alternatives considered:
- Native protocol per runner (no envelope) — rejected: forces every custom runner author to re-derive the same translation layer.
- Wrap stdout as text with markers — rejected: not deterministic, brittle to runner stderr/stdout interleaving.
acceptance_criteria: schemas/custom-runner.schema.json validates as JSON Schema; ah check with runner='custom' invokes the configured executable, parses the envelope (exit_code, passed, findings), and validates structurally; empty findings + exit_code 0 is a valid pass; non-zero exit captures stderr in execution_error findings; envelope-shape violations emit envelope_violation findings.
close_reason: Implemented custom runner plugin protocol in src/adapters/custom.rs with full TDD: envelope parsing, conflict precedence rules, error handling. Updated check.rs to dispatch custom test type and flow envelope findings into check output. Cross-referenced finding schema in custom-runner.schema.json.

title: ah Q7: ah doctor detection and --enable

Section 7 of add-quality-measurement-and-adapters (tasks.md 7.1–7.9).

close_reason: Implemented Q7: ah doctor detection/recommendations and --enable capability writer; all 9 tasks done

title: ah Q3: Python pytest adapter
description:
Section 2 + Section 3 of add-quality-measurement-and-adapters (tasks.md 2.1–2.5, 3.1–3.6).

Absorbs the Q2 adapter trait (formerly 6nm.3, merged per review: the trait is best defined by use; pytest is the first adapter so it owns the abstraction).

Covers:
- Adapter trait in src/adapters/mod.rs: detect, invoke, normalize (define by use)
- Detection precedence chain (config override > project markers > heuristics)
- Adapter dispatch from config in gate runner; detection/invocation isolation
- Python pytest adapter detect (pyproject.toml/pytest.ini/setup.cfg)
- Invoke pytest with JSON output (pytest-json-report or stdout JSON contract)
- Normalize pytest output to Finding records with suggested_action and playbook_command
- Map collection errors, ImportError, fixture failures to test-failing findings with type
acceptance_criteria: On a Python fixture project: ah check invokes pytest, parses JSON output, and emits findings normalized to the agent-action schema; collection errors / ImportError / fixture failures each map to specific test type and finding kind; adapter trait is exercised by this adapter (defined-by-use); detection picks pyproject.toml/pytest.ini/setup.cfg with documented precedence.
notes: Implemented adapter invoke/normalize dispatch for pytest, added detection precedence coverage (configured > manifest > environment > source_import), documented pytest classification scenarios, and added end-to-end tests for pytest import/fixture/collection failures mapping to specific test.type values.
close_reason: Adapter trait, dispatch, and pytest detection/invocation/normalization all implemented and tested

close_reason: already implemented in prior commits (see git log)

title: add-quality-measurement-and-adapters: ah quality signal + language adapters
description:
Implement OpenSpec proposal add-quality-measurement-and-adapters: layer language adapters (pytest/cargo/vitest + custom plugin protocol), agent-facing finding schema, ah doctor --enable, ah explain, and opt-in quality measurement on top of the spec-assertions gate.

Builds on: add-spec-assertions (must be deployed and green first).

Authoritative task breakdown: openspec/changes/add-quality-measurement-and-adapters/tasks.md (78 TDD task items across sections 0-11).

Blocker called out in proposal: [runners.custom.<name>] JSON envelope shape — Section 0 resolves this and gates sections 3-6.

Acceptance: openspec validate add-quality-measurement-and-adapters --strict passes; Python/Rust/TS sample projects all yield zero findings via ah check.
acceptance_criteria: openspec validate add-quality-measurement-and-adapters --strict passes; ah check on Python/Rust/TypeScript fixture projects emits zero findings on green and the documented finding kinds on red; quality_mutation / quality_property / quality_snapshot signals each land independently; ah explain compile-enforces variant-body coverage; ah doctor --enable detects and reports adapter readiness.
close_reason: All 15 subtasks complete, all 82/82 openspec tasks checked off, ah check green

title: Update OpenSpec docs to define CLI tool name as ah
description: Why: the active OpenSpec change still refers to the standalone CLI as 'espectacular', but the intended command name is 'ah'. What: update the proposal, design, tasks, and delta specs so the documented CLI and binary references use 'ah' consistently.

title: Make OpenSpec change add-spec-assertions validate
description: Why: openspec validation currently fails for the add-spec-assertions change because it has no spec delta files. What: add at least one capability delta under openspec/changes/add-spec-assertions/specs/<capability>/spec.md using ADDED, MODIFIED, REMOVED, or RENAMED requirement sections plus at least one Scenario block per requirement, then rerun strict validation.

title: Configure repo with wai, beads, openspec, and dont
description: Why: establish local project configuration and repo-visible workflow scaffolding for wai, bd, openspec, and dont so future sessions have consistent tooling context. What: audit current setup, initialize missing tool state where appropriate, and document the resulting workflow in repo files.

title: F17: espectacular — rename docs.yml workflow name to 'Docs' (from 'Deploy docs'), match 4/5 majority
description:
Source: CLI consistency audit F17

Problem: espectacular's docs.yml uses "name: Deploy docs" while all other repos (wai, dont, pretender, testaruda) use "name: Docs".

Expected: Change the workflow name from "Deploy docs" to "Docs" in espectacular/.github/workflows/docs.yml.

Files: espectacular/.github/workflows/docs.yml — change the "name:" field at the top.

Verification: "head -3 espectacular/.github/workflows/docs.yml | grep 'name: Docs'" should match.

Classification: AFK (agent-friendly).

title: F10: espectacular — add shell completions subcommand (clap_complete)
description:
Source: CLI consistency audit F10

Problem: espectacular has no shell completions subcommand, unlike dont which ships clap_complete-based completions. All CLIs are built on clap, making this a few lines.

Expected: Add "completions" subcommand to ah (matching dont's pattern) using clap_complete — generate completions for bash, zsh, fish, powershell.

Files: src/main.rs or src/cli.rs — add Completions subcommand to clap parser.

Reference: dont is the canonical implementation.

Verification: "ah completions bash > /dev/null" should produce valid bash completions without errors.

Classification: HITL (CLI API change, needs human review).

title: F6: espectacular — align unknown-subcommand exit code to 2 (match suite convention)
description:
Source: CLI consistency audit F6

Problem: "ah bogus" (espectacular) exits 2 (clap default), which matches the suite convention. This issue is about verifying no regressions: document that the exit code is already correct and add a test if one does not exist.

Expected: Confirm "ah bogus; echo $?" returns 2. Add a test asserting this if one doesn't exist yet.

Files: Verify clap's default behaviour. Add test if missing.

Verification: "ah bogus; echo $?" should return 2.

Classification: AFK (agent-friendly).

title: F14: espectacular — add missing scaffold files (_typos.toml, book.toml)
description:
Source: CLI consistency audit F14

Problem: espectacular is missing _typos.toml (typo-checker config) and book.toml (mdBook docs config) that exist in sibling repos. These are copy-once scaffold files.

Expected: Add _typos.toml and book.toml to espectacular/, matching the convention in dont/ (which has both).

Files to create: espectacular/_typos.toml — copy from dont/_typos.toml as reference; espectacular/book.toml — copy from dont/book.toml as reference.

Verification: "test -f espectacular/_typos.toml && test -f espectacular/book.toml" should succeed.

Classification: AFK (agent-friendly).

title: Implement ah report coverage matrix command
description:
## Why this exists

Four cli spec contracts (report-coverage-by-spec-and-archetype, report-exits-non-zero-when-scenarios-are-missing-contracts, report-exits-zero-when-coverage-is-complete, report-json-output) require an 'ah report' command that does not exist. These contracts remain no-tests-declared until the command is implemented.

## What needs to be done

1. Add a Report subcommand to the CLI in main.rs
2. Implement run_report: traverse .espectacular/*/contracts, join with openspec scenarios, produce a coverage matrix per spec and archetype
3. Exit zero when all scenarios have valid passing contracts, non-zero when any are missing
4. Support --json flag to emit a machine-readable conformance matrix
5. Wire up the four contract stubs with [[tests.shell]] entries pointing at integration tests

## Acceptance criteria

ah check reports 0 no-tests-declared for all four report-* contracts in the cli spec

title: Audit 56 passing contracts for correct test filter scope
description:
## Why this exists

ah check reports 56 contracts as passing, but a green contract only proves the declared test command exits zero — not that the test filter actually targets the right behavior. A filter like 'cargo test -q ah_archive_' covers all archive tests regardless of the specific scenario; if future tests are added nearby, a contract could stay green while covering unintended tests.

## What needs to be done

For each passing contract, verify that the test filter (flags/command) is as narrow as possible and actually exercises the scenario named in the contract. Cross-reference the scenario prose with the test body.

## Acceptance criteria

Each passing contract's test filter can be justified by pointing to specific test functions or test names that correspond to the scenario.

title: Enable cargo and pytest adapters (ah doctor recommendation)
description:
## Why this exists

ah doctor reports two actionable recommendations:
  recommendation: pytest detected via environment — run: ah doctor --enable pytest
  recommendation: cargo detected via manifest — run: ah doctor --enable cargo

These adapters are present in the environment but not enabled in .espectacular/config.toml.

## What needs to be done

Run:
  ah doctor --enable pytest
  ah doctor --enable cargo

Then verify with ah doctor that no more recommendations appear.

## Acceptance criteria

ah doctor output shows no recommendation lines.
